plan_adherence_notes: No plan.json was ever created. Research proposed spawning two parallel children (child-gamma: test_gamma.py, child-delta: test_delta.py). Child-delta completed all phases successfully and created backend/test_delta.py matching the established pattern exactly. Child-gamma failed entirely due to CLI errors (worker-output.jsonl shows 'no stdin data' and '--output-format=stream-json requires --verbose') — it never produced questions.json, research.json, or test_gamma.py. Only 1 of 2 planned parallel tasks produced output, so the parallel decomposition test is incomplete.
missed_questions: No questions.json was generated at the parent task level (analysis phase failed with CLI errors)
Child-gamma produced no questions.json or research.json — total child failure
No question about error handling or retry strategy if a parallel child fails
No question about how to verify the parallel tasks actually ran concurrently vs sequentially
reason: The task 'Test parallel-capable task decomposition' is incomplete. While child-delta succeeded fully (created backend/test_delta.py with correct conventions, validated), child-gamma failed entirely due to CLI invocation errors and never created backend/test_gamma.py. The parallel decomposition was partially demonstrated — the system did spawn two children and merge their branches — but only one child produced useful work. The parent task also lacked questions.json and plan.json artifacts. To approve, child-gamma would need to be re-run or the missing test_gamma.py created manually.
